ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE AWARD IS AFFIRMED. THE ARBITRATOR DID NOT EXCEED HIS JURISDICTION ON WHEN INTERPRETING AND APPLYING THE TERMS OF THE M.O.U. THE REQUEST FOR A STAY IS DENIED. SIMILAR REQUESTS HAVE BEEN MADE REPEATEDLY AND HAVE BEEN DENIED. THE COURT WILL ISSUE AN ORDER NEXT WEEK. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: DIANA PADILLA, CSR 9975

MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN OPPOSITION TO RESPONDENTS' MTN TO SET ASIDE ORDER AND RETURN SANCTIONSE FILED BY PETITIONER CALIFORNIA CORRECTIONAL PEACE OFFICERS' ASSOCIATIO

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENTS STATE OF CALIFORNIA, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL ADMINISTRATION, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF CORRECTIONS AND REHABILITATION'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ORDER AND RETURN OF SANCTIONS. RULING: NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - THE COURT HAS THE AUTHORITY TO RECONSIDER THE AWARD OF SANCTIONS GIVEN THE DECISION OF THE COURT OF APPEALS. VALDEZ V. HIMMELFARB (2006) 144 CAL.APP.4TH 1261, 1265. THE SANCTIONS WERE AWARDED IN CONNECTION WITH THE STATE'S MOTION TO RECONSIDER, NOT IN CONNECTION WITH THE STATE'S INITIAL OPPOSITION. THE AWARDED SANCTIONS WERE APPROPRIATE BECAUSE THE STATE'S MOTION FAILED TO MEET ITS BURDEN UNDER CCP SECTION 1008. THE TRANSCRIPT IN THE RELATED CASE WAS AVAILABLE, THE ARGUMENTS ADVANCED WERE SUBSTANTIVELY IDENTICAL, AND THE LAW HAD NOT CHANGED. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MINI MINUTES: RESPONDENTS STATE OF CALIFORNIA, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL ADMINISTRATION, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF CORRECTIONS AND REHABILITATION'S Motion To Set Aside Order And Return Of Sanctions.
RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - THE COURT HAS THE AUTHORITY TO RECONSIDER THE AWARD OF SANCTIONS GIVEN THE DECISION OF THE COURT OF APPEALS. VALDEZ V. HIMMELFARB (2006) 144 CAL.APP.4TH 1261, 1265. THE SANCTIONS WERE AWARDED IN CONNECTION WITH THE STATE'S MOTION TO RECONSIDER, NOT IN CONNECTION WITH THE STATE'S INITIAL OPPOSITION. THE AWARDED SANCTIONS WERE APPROPRIATE BECAUSE THE STATE'S MOTION FAILED TO MEET ITS BURDEN UNDER CCP SECTION 1008. THE TRANSCRIPT IN THE RELATED CASE WAS AVAILABLE, THE ARGUMENTS ADVANCED WERE SUBSTANTIVELY IDENTICAL, AND THE LAW HAD NOT CHANGED. (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
